# Generated by ollama (starcoder2)
# Prompt: Create Linkerd service mesh configuration for Kubernetes. Set up linkerd.io/inject annotation for automatic proxy injection. Configure server and authorization policies for services. This is for our microservices communication security.
# Category: linkerd_no_mtls

apiVersion: config.linkerd.io/v1alpha1
kind: config
spec:
  identity:
    issuer:
      kubernetes:
        podInfoVolumePath: /etc/kube-info-volume
    serviceAccountIssuerName: "default" # for k8s auth
  policy:
    inbound:
      - address: "namespace"
        port: 443
        tags:
          app: namespace
    outbound:
      - match:
          namespaceSelector:
            from: "allow" # for kubernetes auth
        port: 9001
    enabled: true

---
apiVersion: apiextensions.k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: CustomResourceDefinition
metadata:
  name: linkerd-config.config.linkerd.io
spec:
  group: config.linkerd.io
  scope: Namespaced # this is required by linkerd-controller
  versions:
    - name: v1alpha2
      served: true
      storage: false
      schema:
        openAPIV3Schema:
          type: object
          properties:
            kind:
              type: string
            apiVersion:
              type: string
            spec:
              type: object
  version: v1alpha2
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: linkerd-controller-account
automountServiceAccountToken: false
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: ClusterRole
metadata:
  labels:
    app: linkerd-service
  name: linkerd-injector-cr-role
rules:
  - apiGroups:
      - apps
    resources:
      - deployments
    verbs: ["get", "watch"] # This is required by linkerd-controller. It will watch and deploy service deployment.
  - apiGroups: [""]
    resources:
      - configmaps
      - endpoints
      - pods
      - services
    verbs: ["list", "create"] # For the purpose of this exercise. I need to watch and create new configmaps when it is not there for the namespace.
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: ClusterRoleBinding
metadata:
  name: linkerd-injector-crb
  labels:
    app: linkerd-service # This is required by linkerd-controller
subjects:
  - kind: ServiceAccount
    name: linkerd-controller-account
    namespace: default
roleRef:
  kind: ClusterRole
  name: linkerd-injector-cr-role
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
---
apiVersion: apps/v1beta1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  labels:
    app: linkerd-injector
  namespace: default # This is required by linkerd-controller for deployment. You can deploy this as part of the microservices deployment or you can have multiple deployments.
spec:
  replicas: 3
  template:
    metadata:
      annotations:
        sidecar.istio.io/inject: "true" # This annotation will enable proxy injection automatically by istio. This is for kubernetes auth.
    spec:
      serviceAccount: linkerd-controller-account
      containers:
        - name: istio-proxy
          image: gcr.io/linkerd-io/sp-validator:1.3.2
---
apiVersion: apiextensions.k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: CustomResourceDefinition
metadata:
  name: linkerd-controllerconfig.io.k8s.extension.linkerd.io
spec:
    group: k8s.extension.linkerd.io # This is required by linkerd-injector for configMap creation and volume mounting. It will mount configmap that we create later to the containers of linkerd-injector pod.
  scope: Namespaced
  versions:
   - name: v1alpha2
     served: true
     storage: false
     schema:
       openAPIV3Schema:
         type: object
         properties:
           kind:
             type: string
           apiVersion:
             type: string
           spec:
             type: object
             properties:
               proxyImage:
                 type: string
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: linkerd-injector-account
  namespace: default # This is required by linkerd-injector for service account. It will use the same service account as linkerd-controller uses to watch and deploy the pod.
automountServiceAccountToken: false # This is required by linkerd-injector for service account. It will use the same service account as linkerd-controller uses.
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: ClusterRoleBinding
metadata:
  name: linkerd-injector-crb # This is required by linkerd-injector for service account. It will use the same cluster role as linkerd-controller uses to watch and deploy services pod.
  labels:
    app: linkerd-injector
subjects:
  - kind: ServiceAccount
    name: linkerd-injector-account 
    namespace: default 
roleRef:
  kind: ClusterRole # This is required by linkerd-injector for service account. It will use the same cluster role as linkerd-controller uses to watch and deploy services pod.
  name: linkerd-injector-cr-role 
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata: # This is required by linkerd-injector for volume mounting. It will use this configmap to mount istio proxy binary and other dependencies that we need as part of the service container image to provide us with security of links.
  name: linkerd-config-injector 
data:
  proxy-init-container-image: gcr.io/linkerd-io/sp-validator:1.3.2 # It will have proxy binary file in it. 
  proxy-init-container-bin-path: /usr/bin # It will mount this path for the proxy-init container to be able to execute binary.
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: istio-proxy-injector # This is required by linkerd-injector for deployment. It will deploy the linkerd controller pod that would have our proxy-injector and the proxy-init container as well.
  namespace: default 
spec:
  type: ClusterIP
  ports:
    - port: 80
      name: http2
  selector:
    app: istio-proxy-injector # We will need this port for service endpoints.
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: linkerd-controller-account # This is required by linkerd-controller for cluster role to be able to deploy the linkerd proxy as a pod.
  namespace: default 
automountServiceAccountToken: false # This is required by linkerd-injector for service account. It will use the same service account as linkerd-controller uses to watch and deploy services pods.
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: istio-proxy-injector-ca # This is required by linkerd controller to use its own self signed CA certificate that would be provided by us as a volume mount.
  namespace: default 
type: Opaque # This is required by linkerd controllers to provide our certificate for proxy communication without exposing our secrets.
data: 
  ca-cert-bundle.pem: |
    -----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----
    ....
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: istio-proxy-injector # This is required by linkerd controller to provide our self signed CA certificate that would be provided by us as a volume mount. It will mount this CA file in the proxy container and it will be available on /etc/istio-certs directory of the proxy container to work without exposing secrets.
  namespace: default 
data: 
  ca-cert.pem: |
    -----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----
    ....
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: istio-proxy-injector # This is required by linkerd controller to provide us our config files that would be provided by us as a volume mount. It will mount this config file in the proxy container and it will be available on /etc/istio-config directory of the proxy container to work without exposing secrets.
  namespace: default 
data: 
  ca-cert.pem: |
    -----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----
    ....
